aligned.req: CLI tool ralph-admin with 10 commands
aligned.task: Task 1 (CLI stubs with commander), Task 10 (wire all commands to implementations)

aligned.req: Reads ralph state from disk (state.json, inventory.json, rules.toml) — NO code imports
aligned.task: Task 4 (StateReader, InventoryReader, RulesReader) — each reads from file path, parses locally

aligned.req: Wraps PM2 for lifecycle (start, stop, restart, list)
aligned.task: Task 5 (Pm2Client) — wraps pm2.connect/list/start/stop/restart/delete

aligned.req: Scaffolds new loops — creates state dir, rules.toml, inventory.json
aligned.task: Task 9 (ScaffoldBuilder) — scaffoldStateDir creates dir + rules.toml + inventory.json

aligned.req: Injects working-directory header into _GOAL file
aligned.task: Task 7 (GoalFileManager) — injectHeader with idempotent check for '## Working Directory'

aligned.req: Tracks progress — reads inventory phases/tasks, displays completion percentage
aligned.task: Task 4 (InventoryReader.completionPercentage, formatProgress, countByStatus)

aligned.req: Detects anomalies — crash-looping, stuck, completed-but-still-running
aligned.task: Task 8 (Doctor) — diagnose() with CRASH_LOOP_THRESHOLD=100, STUCK_THRESHOLD=10, COMPLETED_THRESHOLD=100

aligned.req: OOP classes per domain: Pm2Client, StateReader, InventoryReader, RulesManager, ScaffoldBuilder, GoalFileManager, CliRouter
aligned.task: All classes present: Pm2Client (Task 5), StateReader (Task 4), InventoryReader (Task 4), RulesReader (Task 4, renamed from RulesManager), ScaffoldBuilder (Task 9), GoalFileManager (Task 7), CliRouter via Commander (Task 1)

aligned.req: DRY — shared base for reading; PM2 ops via single wrapper
aligned.task: Schemas defined once in Task 2, each reader is separate class, Pm2Client is single PM2 wrapper

aligned.req: Performance — lazy reads, Promise.all, cache PM2 list per invocation
aligned.task: Readers read on demand (Task 4), Pm2Client.listAll caches per call, Task 10 wiring uses Promise.all for parallel state+inventory reads

aligned.req: Fail-closed — doctor shows problems, start validates state dir
aligned.task: Doctor defaults to surfacing anomalies (Task 8), start resolves config before PM2 launch (Task 10)

aligned.req: Separation of concerns — never writes to state.json, only writes rules.toml, inventory.json (scaffold), _GOAL header
aligned.task: ScaffoldBuilder only writes rules.toml + inventory.json (Task 9), GoalFileManager writes _GOAL header (Task 7), StateReader/InventoryReader are read-only (Task 4)

aligned.req: Separate repository — ralph-admin in own repo
aligned.task: §0 Architecture Decision Record: buihongduc132/ralph-admin (NEW)

aligned.req: File format contracts defined locally (not imported)
aligned.task: Task 2: RalphState, Inventory, RulesTOML schemas defined in src/schemas/

aligned.req: List output format with columns NAME/STATUS/ITER/MODEL/PROGRESS/UPTIME/RESTARTS
aligned.task: Task 6 (Formatter.formatListTable)

aligned.req: Doctor output format with CRASH-LOOPING/COMPLETED-BUT-RUNNING/HEALTHY categories
aligned.task: Task 6 (Formatter.formatDoctorOutput) + Task 8 (Doctor.diagnose)

aligned.req: Convention resolution (name → paths)
aligned.task: Task 3 (LoopConfig) — resolves pm2Name, stateDir, stateFile, inventoryFile, rulesFile, worktreePath, goalFile, branch from name

aligned.req: Tech stack: Bun, pm2 npm, smol-toml, commander, bun:test
aligned.task: Task 1 Step 2 installs pm2, smol-toml, commander; tsconfig targets ES2022 with bun-types

aligned.req: Build: bun build → single binary bin/ralph-admin
aligned.task: Task 11 — bun build src/cli.ts --compile --outfile bin/ralph-admin

aligned.req: _GOAL header injection is idempotent
aligned.task: Task 7 — GoalFileManager.injectHeader checks for '## Working Directory', returns {skipped: true} if present

aligned.req: Scaffold writes rules.toml with defaults (I%5 sync, I%7 backward, I%11 deep review, I%15 guard)
aligned.task: Task 9 — DEFAULT_RULES_TOML includes entries at 5, 7, 11, 15 with matching prompts

aligned.req: Scaffold writes inventory.json with empty phases/tasks structure
aligned.task: Task 9 — ScaffoldBuilder writes {lastUpdated, currentPhase: '', phases: {}}

aligned.req: Scaffold is idempotent — does not overwrite existing files
aligned.task: Task 9 — checks existsSync before writing; test verifies existing content preserved

gaps.req: Scaffold step 1: Create git worktree via 'git worktree add ../repo-wt-my-feature -b wt/my-feature'
gaps.detail: Intention scaffold step 1 explicitly requires git worktree creation. Plan's ScaffoldBuilder (Task 9) only creates state dir + files. No git worktree creation code or test exists. Config (Task 3) resolves worktree path but doesn't create it.

gaps.req: Scaffold step 6: Output the ralph start command with correct --state-dir, --no-commit, --reuse-state flags
gaps.detail: Intention says scaffold should 'Output the ralph start command with correct flags' after creating everything. Plan's Task 10 scaffold wiring doesn't mention printing the start command. Users won't know what to run next.

gaps.req: Risk mitigation: 'Version field in state file; ralph-admin warns on unknown version'
gaps.detail: Intention's risk table requires a version field in RalphState and a warning when unknown version encountered. Plan's RalphState schema (§1.1) has no version field. No warning logic anywhere in plan.
Intention's risk table says 'Pin pm2 version'. Plan's Task 1 Step 2 installs pm2 without version pin. No .nvmrc or engines field.

gaps.req: Start step 5: Verify process started (check pid)
gaps.detail: Intention's Start Behavior step 5 says 'Verify process started (check pid)'. Plan's Pm2Client.start() resolves on callback but doesn't verify the process is actually running afterward.

gaps.req: Concurrent safety: scaffold should warn/error if ralph process already running for that name
gaps.detail: Intention says 'only writes during scaffold (before ralph starts)' but plan has no guard against scaffolding while a ralph loop is already running for that name.

gold_plating.task: Task 6: Formatter class with pad(), formatStatus() helper functions
gold_plating.note: Traces to List Output and Doctor Output format requirements. Not gold-plating — intention specifies exact column layout.

gold_plating.task: Task 3: LoopConfig with resolution order (PM2 args → convention → CLI flags)
gold_plating.note: Traces to convention resolution implied by all commands. Intention mentions 'resolve from name convention'. Three-tier resolution is reasonable engineering.
Traces to 'Build: bun build → single binary' in Tech Stack section.

checks.separate_repo: PASS — Plan specifies buihongduc132/ralph-admin as new repo
checks.oop_no_god_file: PASS — 11 classes across separate files, none > 150 lines
checks.dry: PASS — Schemas defined once in src/schemas/, readers don't duplicate parsing logic
checks.pm2_wrapper: PASS — Pm2Client wraps all pm2 programmatic API calls
checks.filesystem_contract: PASS — RalphState, Inventory, RulesTOML schemas defined locally with TypeScript interfaces
checks.naming_mismatch: NOTE — Intention says 'RulesManager' but plan calls it 'RulesReader'. Functionally equivalent (both read rules.toml), but naming diverges.

performance.lazy_reads: PASS — All readers construct with file path, read() called on demand
performance.promise_all: PASS — Task 10 wiring mentions Promise.all for parallel state+inventory reads
performance.pm2_connection_per_command: PASS — Pm2Client.connect() is lazy, disconnect() called in finally block per command
performance.note: No explicit caching of PM2 process list within a single command invocation mentioned in Task 5. listAll() makes a fresh call each time. If list + doctor both call listRalph(), they make separate PM2 calls. Minor — could be optimized later.

evidence: All default values specified: --model bhd-litellm/role-smart, --agent pi, --max-iterations 999
All thresholds defined: CRASH_LOOP_THRESHOLD=100, STUCK_THRESHOLD=10, COMPLETED_THRESHOLD=100
All output formats specified with exact columns and ANSI codes
CLI parsing via commander — no interactive prompts
No 'choose this or that' language in any task step

file_format_contracts.ralph_state: PASS — §1.1 defines RalphState interface with all fields intention mentions
file_format_contracts.inventory: PASS — §1.2 defines Inventory with phases, tasks, status enum
file_format_contracts.rules_toml: PASS — §1.3 defines RulesToml with rules and state_injection
file_format_contracts.note: Missing version field in RalphState (see gaps). Intention's risk table requires it for drift detection.

evidence: GoalFileManager.injectHeader checks content.includes('## Working Directory') before injecting
Returns {skipped: true, injected: false} if header present
Test 'idempotent — skips if header already present' verifies this explicitly

intention_steps: 1. Create worktree: git worktree add ../repo-wt-my-feature -b wt/my-feature
6. Output ralph start command with correct flags
plan_coverage: Step 1: MISSING — ScaffoldBuilder does not create git worktree
Step 6: MISSING — no 'print start command' step after scaffold

issues: CRITICAL: Scaffold does not create git worktree (intention step 1). ScaffoldBuilder only creates state dir. Users would need to manually create worktree before running scaffold, which defeats the purpose.
HIGH: Scaffold does not output the ralph start command after completion (intention step 6). Users won't know what command to run next.
MEDIUM: RalphState schema missing version field for drift detection. Intention risk table explicitly requires 'Version field in state file; ralph-admin warns on unknown version'.
MEDIUM: Start command doesn't verify process started (no pid check). Intention step 5 requires verification.
LOW: PM2 version not pinned. Intention risk mitigation says 'Pin pm2 version'.
LOW: No guard against scaffolding while ralph loop is already running for that name.

remediation.issue: Missing git worktree creation in scaffold
remediation.fix: Add worktree creation step to ScaffoldBuilder or a new GitWorktreeManager class: execSync(`git worktree add ${worktreePath} -b ${branch}`). Add test with mocked git. Update Task 9 scaffold test to verify worktree creation.

remediation.issue: Missing start command output after scaffold
remediation.fix: After scaffold completes, print the exact ralph start command with resolved paths: `ralph-dev --agent pi --model bhd-litellm/role-smart ... --state-dir .ralph-{name} --no-commit --reuse-state --prompt-file '{goalFile}'`. Add to Task 10 scaffold wiring.
Add `version?: number;` to RalphState schema (§1.1). Add version check in StateReader.read() — warn on stderr if version > 1 or missing.
After Pm2Client.start(), call listAll() and findByName() to confirm process is online with a valid pid. Throw if not found within 5s.
Pin in package.json: `"pm2": "5.x"` (or current major). Add engines field.
